_comment: Auto-generated by scripts/build-draft-articles-json.py. Do not hand-edit — your changes will be overwritten on next deploy. To add a draft: drop articles/draft-{slug}.html (or set draft:true in articles.json) and run `make drafts`.

drafts.title: Yorkshire Pudding: The Perfect Version
drafts.lede: Smoking beef dripping at 230°C. Cold high-hydration bread-flour batter into hot fat. Sealed oven, no peeking. Yorkshire pudding done by physics.

drafts.title: What Littoralicious Stands For — The Three Pillars, Written Down
drafts.lede: Three pillars, eight principles, one mission. The DNA of Littoralicious — the grandmother, the scientist, the yacht chef — written down so every article answers to the same standard. With the banned words, the voice rules, the design lock, the 11-PM test that every piece must pass before publication.

drafts.title: Venice in Ten Days: The Lagoon Window That Justifies the Trip
drafts.lede: The ten days when castraure and moeche overlap in the Venetian lagoon. The shortest seasonal window in European cooking — and what it demands of a yacht chef who happens to be in port.

drafts.title: Roast Lamb: The Perfect Version (Overnight Shoulder)
drafts.lede: Bone-in shoulder, dry-rubbed, slow-roast at 110°C overnight to 90°C internal. Final 220°C blast for the crackle. Shoulder only — leg uses a different recipe.

drafts.title: Field Report Part 1 — Ten Frontier Techniques From Outside the Kitchen
drafts.lede: Ten frontier techniques whose source is not a restaurant or a chef — from 5,000-year-old Andean solar freeze-drying to Hiperbaric high-pressure processing, from Kishu white-charcoal craft to Ajinomoto's transglutaminase patents, from Charles Spence's Oxford psychology lab to Atlantic Sapphire's land-based salmon farm, from GreenWave kelp arrays to Solar Foods' air-protein bioreactors and Kew's DNA-barcoded plant database. Each section: the source, the move, the science, the galley translation.

drafts.title: Field Report — Ten Techniques From the Frontier Labs
drafts.lede: Ten techniques from the labs and restaurants writing the next decade of cooking — Noma Projects on fermentation stacks, Aponiente on marine biomass, Etxebarri on single-fire discipline, Spence and Blumenthal on sound as a flavour input, Alchemist on multisensory plating, Central on vertical ecosystem menus, Geranium and Tartine on point-of-use stone-milling, and Pic / Mirazur / Frantzén on Pacojet cryotextures. Each section: the lab, the move, the science, what it teaches, the galley translation.

drafts.title: The 730-Plate Problem — A Working Method for Cooking for Crew
drafts.lede: A yacht chef serves 730 plates per crew member per year — same nine faces, no menu to hide behind. The mistake is to call it casual. The opportunity is touchstone-driven cooking: named real-world anchors, a weekly cadence built on Blue-Zone weekdays and ugly-delicious Fridays, and the nationality plates that make a crew member text their mother.

drafts.title: Moist Carrot Cake: The Science-First Version
drafts.lede: Toasted-walnut butter, macerated carrot, mascarpone-tempered cream cheese frosting. Three science-backed modifications that produce the moistest, most stable carrot cake you will ever serve on charter. Every step tested, every claim sourced.

drafts.title: Battered Fish: The Perfect Version
drafts.lede: Cold beer, cold vodka, cold batter into 185°C oil. Rice flour for shatter, plain for cling, baking soda for lace. Fish and chips done by physics.

drafts.title: Chef Agent v5.0: One Prompt That Runs Your Whole Menu Department
drafts.lede: A 919-line prompt that handles every yacht menu service — eight formats, ten cuisines, guest preferences, wine pairing, and the LaTeX-to-PDF pipeline.

drafts.title: Inside the Test Kitchens — Twelve Food Labs Setting the Direction of Cooking
drafts.lede: Twelve food labs — Noma Projects, the elBulli Foundation, Mater Iniciativa at Central, Aponiente, Mugaritz, Alchemist, Modernist Cuisine, Pujol, Disfrutar, BCC Innovation, Atelier Crenn, Geranium — and what each one is currently working on. The R&D infrastructure of fine dining, surveyed for the working chef who wants to steal one transferable practice from each.

drafts.title: Sous Vide Vegetables — The Bag You Open When Onshore Lunch Falls Through
drafts.lede: Eleven sous vide vegetable bags — built once a fortnight, held at −18°C for months, deployed in twenty-five minutes when onshore lunch is cancelled at 11:32. White asparagus in brown butter and parmigiano, artichoke barigoule, beetroot confit, and the Mediterranean rescue menu they assemble.

drafts.title: The Crew Pharmacy — Twelve Food Combinations Where the Pairing Does the Healing
drafts.lede: Twelve food combinations where the pairing does the healing — turmeric and black pepper for anti-inflammatory relief, tomato and olive oil for sun defense, tart cherry and walnut for sleep, ginger and mint for motion sickness. The crew cook as quiet medic, with one weekly rotation and a galley pharmacy drawer that fits in a bread tin.

drafts.title: Yorkshire Pudding — Galley Card
drafts.lede: One-page galley card for Yorkshire pudding at 230°C with smoking beef dripping.

drafts.title: Roast Lamb Shoulder, Overnight — Galley Card
drafts.lede: One-page galley card for overnight roast lamb shoulder.

drafts.title: Battered Fish — Galley Card
drafts.lede: One-page galley card for battered fish at 185°C.

drafts.title: Victoria Sponge
drafts.lede: Reverse-creamed sponge, equal-weight ratio, cultured butter, hand-mashed raspberry jam. The Victoria sponge as the science says it should be built — level top, velvet crumb, caster sugar dust, not icing.

drafts.title: Pizza Napoletana: Poolish, 48h Cold Ferment, Broiler Finish
drafts.lede: Poolish, 48-hour cold ferment, 65% hydration, broiler-steel finish. The science-first Neapolitan, every modification sourced. Six panetti, six pizzas.

drafts.title: Machine Hollandaise: Vitamix & Thermomix, One Recipe
drafts.lede: Hollandaise built in a Vitamix or a Thermomix. Clarified butter, acid-first reduction, 70 to 72 degree pasteurisation hold. One sauce, two machines, four hours of holding stability.

drafts.title: Port Call Split: The Dalmatian Hub Playbook
drafts.lede: Port Call Split: ACI + Kaštela + Lav, Ribarnica at 06:30, Pazar at the Palace wall, the local-favorite Sućidar pazar in Plokite, neighbourhood butchers (Certa, Škokić) + fish shops (Kantar Stobreč, Gastro Brač) the locals use, METRO for the bulk run, Pelješac wineries + Brač olive oil. Watch-outs for charter Saturday, Bura, Croatian VAT.

drafts.title: The Rational Is Your Sous-Chef: A Sole-Chef Operating Manual
drafts.lede: One sole chef, twelve guests, six crew, one Rational on the wall. The eight programmes most chefs ignore — Steam, 300C combi, overnight probe, Croissants, Roast Chicken, Finishing, iLevelControl, auto-clean — turned into a daily operating manual.

drafts.title: Build Your Own Recipe Library: Cookbooks, Open Archives, and AI
drafts.lede: How to build a personal recipe library from the best cookbooks using open archives and AI. Search across Modernist Cuisine, The Food Lab, and ChefSteps in seconds. Extract, compare, and forge your own science-backed recipes.

drafts.title: The Locker: Deep Pantry Guides for Chefs Who Stock Like an Arsenal
drafts.lede: The Locker: deep pantry guides for chefs who stock like an arsenal. Specific brands, specific reasons, three tiers. Coming soon.

drafts.title: Signal Fire: One Chef, One Idea That Changes Everything
drafts.lede: Signal Fire: one chef, one idea that changes everything. Not a profile. A dissection of a single principle that survived decades in kitchens. Coming soon.

drafts.title: Port Call: Provisioning Guides Built by the Chefs Who Actually Dock There
drafts.lede: Port Call is the provisioning playbook series: three berths, real suppliers with phone + address, inline cautions, watch-outs by season. Venice + Miami live. St. Barth, Antibes, Palma incoming.
